name: Order barriers
statement_text:
If an explicit s-stage Runge–Kutta method has order p, then
s ≥ p.
proof_text:
Proof. Let t = [[· · · [t] · · · ]] such that r(t) = p > s. The order condition
associated with this tree is Φ(t) = 1/γ(t), where γ(t) = p! and Φ(t) = b Ap−1 1.
Because A is strictly lower triangular, Ap = 0. Hence, the order condition
becomes 0 = 1/p!, which has no solution.
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
The theorem concerns explicit Runge--Kutta methods, where $s$ is the number of stages and $p$ is the order. The preceding text discusses constructing explicit methods with $s = p$ for $p$ up to $4$, and notes that $s < p$ is impossible.

variables.name: s
variables.definition: number of stages in an explicit Runge–Kutta method

variables.name: p
variables.definition: order of an explicit Runge–Kutta method
